playbook.intent: Open red-flag risk -> joint decision + recorded next step the stakeholder will own.
playbook.scope: A PM has spotted a slip, scope ambiguity, or commitment risk on a live engagement. This playbook runs a single escalation conversation: framing, evidence presentation, options, decision capture, and follow-through hook. Done when both sides can repeat the next step out loud and the decision is logged in the project record.

success-criteria.criterion: Written decision captured by end of meeting with named owner
Stakeholder restates the agreed next step unprompted
Risk register updated within 24 hours with new status
Follow-up checkpoint scheduled before the meeting ends

stage.name: Prep
stage.intent: Walk in with evidence, not feelings.

tasks.task: Pull risk + change-control history for the affected scope
Draft 2-3 concrete options ranked by trade-off
Pre-write the decision template (who decides, by when, what)

stage.decision-gate: Advance only if the brief states the ask in one sentence and the options are mutually exclusive. Otherwise rewrite.

stage.name: Frame
stage.intent: Name the problem in the first 60 seconds, no stalling.

tasks.task: Open with the single sentence ask
Surface the impact tied to a metric the stakeholder cares about
Avoid blame language; state facts and the missing decision

outputs.output: Shared problem statement acknowledged by stakeholder

stage.decision-gate: Advance when the stakeholder restates the problem in their own words. If they re-frame it as "minor", restart with stronger evidence.

stage.name: Options
stage.intent: Present 2-3 paths with the cost of each.

tasks.task: Walk each option: cost, time, scope impact, residual risk
Invite a fourth option from the stakeholder

outputs.output: Option matrix with stakeholder reactions noted

stage.decision-gate: Advance when one option has clearly emerged or stakeholder commits to choose by a stated time. Do not leave with "we will think about it".

stage.name: Decide
stage.intent: Force a decision in the room, with the owner named.

tasks.task: Fill the decision template live on screen
Name the decision owner, deadline, and rollback condition
Read it back; ask if anything is wrong

outputs.output: Signed decision (verbal or written) with owner + deadline

stage.decision-gate: Advance only if the decision is concrete enough that the next person in the chain could act on it without asking you a follow-up question.

stage.name: Close
stage.intent: Lock the follow-up so the decision does not rot.

tasks.task: Schedule the checkpoint before leaving the call
Capture the action items with owners + due dates
Confirm escalation path if the decision needs to change

stage.decision-gate: Stage is done when both parties can name the date + time of next checkpoint without checking calendars.

stage.name: Log
stage.intent: Make the decision survive turnover and time.

tasks.task: Update risk register entry status + decision link
Post summary in the project channel within 24h

stage.decision-gate: Done when register reflects the new status and the post has at least one acknowledgment from a senior stakeholder.

extra.value: [Treating the meeting as a status update -> no decision extracted, Presenting only the recommendation -> stakeholder feels railroaded, Skipping the post-meeting log -> decision becomes 'he said / she said']
['Could the stakeholder explain the decision to their boss without you?', 'Is the follow-up checkpoint already on the calendar?', 'Is the risk register reflecting the new status?']

{human: faion get-content run-an-escalation-conversation-with-a-stakeholder --format md, agent: faion get-content run-an-escalation-conversation-with-a-stakeholder --format context, json: faion get-content run-an-escalation-conversation-with-a-stakeholder --format json}
[{version: 1.0.0, date: 2026-05-20, note: 'Initial draft from F-059-063 chunk-05, role-project-manager atomic.'}]
